ops.title: Primary cabin preference has no reach across cost tiers
ops.description: Live canonical-ask run (2026-07-14, warm-beachy demo trip, neutral profile): with `preferences.cabin {value: business, priority: primary}` pinned per the C4 parse table, all six finalists came back economy, each annotated "N min outside your preferred cabin" — while five all-J round-trip journeys existed in expand.json (american 57.5k+57.5k LAX-LHR-CMN/CMN-MAD-PHL-LAX; aeroplan 90k+90k CAI; alaska 110k + american 57.5k mixed). Cost-lane dominance + lexicographic-within-tier means a primary cabin preference can never lift preferred-cabin journeys past cheaper off-cabin tiers into the presentation cut, and assess selected 0 notable_stretches (its brief keys notability to "excellence outweighs a named preference miss" — the J journeys have no cabin miss, so the mechanism that surfaces beyond-the-cut journeys skipped exactly the journeys the ask wanted). Net: the engine's answer to "cheap points tickets for business class" is an all-economy board unless the user confirms a cabin constraint. Worth a doctrine look: either fit-primary participates in the cost banding, or notable_stretches also triggers on beyond-the-cut journeys that CLEAR a primary preference the finalists all miss.

ops.text: the canonical warm-beachy ask with preferences.cabin business/primary surfaces at least one all-business journey on the board (as finalist or notable stretch) when such journeys exist in expand.json
